insights.headline: The automated estimate explains a solid share of which posts bring larger gifts (fit score 0.62 on a math-transformed dollar scale).
insights.lede: Typical attributed gift in this file is about PHP 4,557. 343 posts raised far less than the pattern suggested—worth asking what was different about timing, audience, or story.

prediction_cards.label: How much of the pattern the estimate captured
prediction_cards.hint: 1.0 = perfect; 0 = useless. (Technical name: R-squared on log dollars.)

prediction_cards.label: Typical miss on the log-dollar scale
prediction_cards.hint: Smaller is better; think of it as average error before converting back to pesos.

prediction_cards.label: Biggest clue in the automated estimate
prediction_cards.hint: The tool relied on this factor most when guessing gift size

cause_cards.title: Strong linear signal: post type EventPromotion
cause_cards.body: In a straightforward regression, this moves estimated gifts with strength -4.667 (confidence p=0.000). Good for storytelling; still not proof you can move money by tweaking one lever.

cause_cards.title: Only pre-publish facts go into the estimate
cause_cards.body: We deliberately exclude likes, shares, and reach so the score mirrors what your team knows before hitting “post.”

cause_cards.title: Two ways to read the tabs below
cause_cards.body: The “simple statistics” tab is easiest to quote in a meeting. The “automated estimate” tab is tuned for accuracy.

calls_to_action: Post #335 (TikTok/ImpactStory): expected about PHP 835,785 but raised 51,888—compare messaging and timing.
Post #125 (YouTube/ImpactStory): expected about PHP 616,150 but raised 228,690—compare messaging and timing.
Post #187 (TikTok/ImpactStory): expected about PHP 416,301 but raised 250,327—compare messaging and timing.
